Codex/Entry-2/Ghost-Sites-Operations-Arc.title: Ghost Sites Operations Archive
Codex/Entry-2/Ghost-Sites-Operations-Arc.content:
Ghost Sites Operations Archive
PRIVATE CLUB OPERATIONS ARCHIVE
ONGOING OPERATIONS


Banana Boom
Status: Ongoing
Client: Steinway Agriculture
Mission: Capture plantation station and interrogate personnel. If genetic modification claims are confirmed, secure the station and hand it over.
Location: Agri Station 18-56b
Tactical: Blue Company, Golden Company, Spiderling cybertech team.


Red Gambit
Status: Ongoing / Top Secret
Client: Shanxue Industry
Mission: False-flag customer service operation on station ST60 in Jupiter ring.
Objective: Disguise as USOL operators, breach the station, create mayhem, and trigger a political response while keeping overall personnel losses under the agreed threshold.
Tactical: Ember Company, The Bull squad, Terminus squad, Crazy Rabbit shuttlecraft.


UPCOMING OPERATIONS

Bunny Riot — deep-space cruise ship rescue after a crew breakdown.
Convoy F-150.2 — regular lunar convoy protection from production site to launch pad.
Terminal Sweep — termination of several ghost sites and all affiliates.
Raven Claw — acquisition and transport of a new breacher-class shuttlecraft.
The Trials — recruitment campaign on prison asteroid Omega-15.
Addictive Fans — psyop and crowd control for a VIP customer.
The Queen — security detail for the inauguration of Evangelina as pirate queen of the Venus trade route.

Codex/Entry-2/Incident-Overview-Report.title: Incident Overview Report
Codex/Entry-2/Incident-Overview-Report.content:
Incident Overview Report
INCIDENT RESPONSE REPORT
Case Number: 9912-47-L
Reporting Unit: Outer Transit Patrol
Date: [REDACTED]
Time of Call: 03:18
Time of Arrival: 03:47
Location:
Unregistered transit route outside residential sectors.
No permanent infrastructure nearby.
Reason for Call:
Unidentified explosion reported by automated traffic sensors.
Secondary confirmation received from orbital monitoring.
Upon Arrival:
— Area secured
— No active fire detected
— No signs of ongoing threat
Scene Description:
The remains of a transport vehicle were found scattered across the site.
Multiple human remains were present in the surrounding area.
Due to the condition of the bodies, immediate identification was not possible.
No civilians were located at or near the scene.
No witnesses were identified.
Preliminary Assessment:
Damage pattern is consistent with an explosive event.
The origin of the explosion could not be determined on site.
Actions Taken:
— Area cordoned off
— Remains collected for laboratory analysis
— Vehicle fragments documented
— Incident logged for follow-up review
Conclusion:
No immediate public safety risk detected.
The incident appears isolated.
Case status:
Pending laboratory results.
Report filed and forwarded to appropriate departments.

Codex/Entry-2/Interrogation-Jack-Full-Tr.title: Interrogation: Jack — Full Transcript
Codex/Entry-2/Interrogation-Jack-Full-Tr.content:
Interrogation: Jack — Full Transcript
INTERROGATION: JACK
Target: James “Jack” Curtis Fung
Interrogator: Peter Dunk, PC Internal Affairs
Location: Luna, Gargoyle launch site
Interrogator:
Listen, Jack, we both know that you fucked up. All you had to do was bring that girl here, to us. Instead you got drunk, or took something, and lost her. What’s up, bro?
Jack:
I am not your bro. I know what you are trying to do. Not my first rodeo. Fuck you and your company mates. You all are scum.
Interrogator:
All right. Then let’s get real. Where is the girl and who do you work for?
Jack:
Eat shit. I’m not telling you anything.
Interrogator:
Call Mr. Donovan in.
[comm channel opened]
[a person enters]
Interrogator:
Mr. Donovan will inject you with the same substance you were supposed to help deliver some time ago. Two questions. One: who do you work for. Two: where is the girl.
[Donovan injects the substance]
Jack:
This will not work.
Interrogator:
We will see.
[several minutes pass]
Interrogator:
Who do you work for?
Jack:
I… I… energy…
Interrogator:
Again.
Jack:
QQ Energy… what… no…
Interrogator:
Fine. Now where is the girl?
Jack:
I… do… not know… no…
Donovan:
The substance took effect. Either he answers or he does not. Increasing the dose is pointless.
Interrogator:
Wrap this up. Pack him and head straight to Camp Vernon. This site does not belong to us anymore. Destroy all evidence and evacuate.

Codex/Entry-2/Interrogation-Protocol.title: Interrogation Protocol
Codex/Entry-2/Interrogation-Protocol.content:
Interrogation Protocol


title: INTERROGATION PROTOCOL - 10.19.2231
author: Classified
date: 10.19.2231

INTERROGATION PROTOCOL
DOCUMENT
DATE: 10.19.2231

RECORDING
LOCATION: -CLASSIFIED-, MAXIMUM SECURITY.
INTERROGATING OFFICER: [REDACTED]
PERSON OF INTEREST: Zachary Plox, G-RACK ON-SITE CTO.

OFFICER:
Mr Plox, let’s start with a simple fact. This is not a simple interrogation. You are not just a suspect. Consider this your trial.
You and your company have killed hundreds of people, and we are still estimating actual losses.
ZACHARY:
That was not me ! Not us ! I …
OFFICER:
Stop this, please. I ask kindly, but just once. We have checked all the logs, all the system parameters and configuration files. We have cross-referenced them with the default configuration you were supposed to use … and we have found changes, approved by you.
Now, would you like to say something about that ?
ZACHARY:
IT WAS NOT ME ! I DID NOT CHANGE ANYTHING ! smack Ugh !
OFFICER:
I told you, stop that. Speak like a man or this will get ugly, real fast. You have failed to deliver and you have set us up with that shit show. So if you continue to insist that you did nothing … there are other people who can answer questions. More valuable people.
ZACHARY:
I understand, I am sorry.
OFFICER:
Finally. So, I repeat my question - do you know anything about it ?
ZACHARY:
Yes, we have experienced technical issues with the asteroid launch program, so the tech crew proposed some changes to the launch sequence that required my approval. I do not know what exactly they did.
OFFICER:
Oh, I know what they did, and I will tell you. They have altered the asteroids collision course so instead of passing near the colony they were supposed to hit it, causing massive damage and deaths of many influential people. But that did not work actually.
ZACHARY:
What do you mean ? Did the system deflect that attack ?
OFFICER:
Oh, no, sadly. Your “system”, as you call it, is a copy-cat attempt to clone an early version of a Holoscene AI, meaning it had all the bugs plus the ones that your ass-handed engineers added while trying to “fix it”. I honestly have no clue how you were able to make it pass the inspection and where you got the initial code (we will deal with that later).
So instead of just failing, it basically crashed, inverting the programmed flight patterns and the asteroids, instead of crashing the colony, went straight to the ships nearby.
Accidently, the “defence” part of your system operated properly and destroyed MOST of the incoming asteroids. But that was obviously not enough as the ones that passed through headed towards the ships. Some of them crashed, making the crew panic and starting to maneuver. And then your “system” blew them up as the hostile targets. Which means that IFF did not work either, by the way.
So as you see, we know everything already, and you are alive for only one reason - I need to hear the names of those “engineers” that proposed the initial changes.
ZACHARY:
Simon Walls, Freya Kurkwurm, Sergey Plox, and some guy Alexey.
OFFICER:
Who is that Alexey ?
ZACHARY:
I do not know. He was transferred from the main office 2 days prior to the show.
OFFICER:
Did you check his credentials before allowing him access ?
ZACHARY:
Yes, but he came with a letter from the main office and we were in a rush.
OFFICER:
So you are saying that you have neglected security because of the tight time frame. Is that your defence ?
ZACHARY:
No … yes … I don’t know. I am sorry … please … slap
OFFICER:
Please don’t test my patience. There is none left. You will be transferred to one of our facilities for … additional questioning. Oh, and one more thing. There is no “Alexey”, but we have found a manifest embedded in the configuration file. I will let you read it while you are in transit. Good luck.

END OF RECORDING

Codex/Entry-2/Investigation-Result.title: Investigation Result
Codex/Entry-2/Investigation-Result.content:
Investigation Result
Captain Cassius Maelstrom, Lunar sector, CSCH (council security central hub)
Due to the lack of facts, we have not been able to determine the fate of Miss Flanders.
At the same time, unofficially, we could tell the family that there were traces of their daughter’s DNA on those barrels. It is highly likely that the acid was used for body dissolvement.
Our analysts think that she was not the only victim and that “Jack” individual, who we suppose IS James Fung, might be a serial killer. His location is unknown.
The “Sun Hog” representative claimed that no person under that name has been working with them. More to it, he had not found any similarities between the photo of the culprit and working personnel.
Without the main suspect and a victim’s body we are sadly obliged to set the case as “awaiting resolution”, and set both Miss Flanders and James Fung as MIA.
In regards to that James “Jack” Fung guy - he seems to be a veteran of many conflicts, suffering from PTSD or something similar. His remaining team mates speak highly of him but mention him being unstable in stressful situations.
His military record is non-accessible (classified). 
Case status set - COLD.

Codex/Entry-2/JJ_PRIVATE-Archive-Aurana-.title: JJ_PRIVATE Archive: Aurana Emergence
Codex/Entry-2/JJ_PRIVATE-Archive-Aurana-.content:
JJ_PRIVATE Archive: Aurana Emergence
Transformation Logs
I keep trying to place the exact moment everything changed.. when everything became unreal.. it’s not when the alarm sounded and it’s not when they forced me to the floor..
this is all much bigger than that, much bigger than that.. it was the instant I realized I am not a person, but a variable just like everyone else, and i moved out of my expected range..
when you are a child, you take the way things are for granted almost, growing up in that place was growing up believing it was a fortress of intelligence and progress.. I mean that’s what you kept hearing so that’s what you believe about the world.. even its cold walls felt safe and reassuring when I was younger.. cold meant precise and precise meant safe.. safe from the other, safe from the unnamed evils that lurk outside of that same safety..
but when i felt that cold hand on me, when they kept repeating and repeating and repeating procedural phrases instead of my name.. these guys I know, these guys who have known me, something I have been in denial of was just no longer avoidable.. I have spent months figuring out the details and years before that deprogramming my brain, but you have a sense of loyalty in you for these places, for home, an idea it can change at least..
Jack had told me i needed to think rationally, he’s been saying a lot the last weeks that just doesn’t make sense, that he is worried about me, that he was worried about me and I was spiraling.. it’s strange since he was the one who initially started opening my eyes to all this.. stay calm he kept saying.. calm explanations are just a way of controlling the narrative through our naive human craving of comfort.. but Jack did far worse than that, the very person i thought was holding my hand through this mess might have been causing it.. I could tell by how he looked at me.. how he answered.. he denied it, but his face couldn’t.. I can’t get his face out of my head, the frame of him standing in that garage, the weight of his dishonesty hanging around us and holding me in place.. no longer.
there is a deafening silence that follows you when you step outside yourself and your life.. I have stopped expecting the familiar sounds, no more chimes announcing opening and closing doors, no more clawing of filtered air humming through sealed corridors.. Now there are only temporary beds, borrowed clothes.. I am going through everything that has happened again like a passage i looked over without reading, something so obvious and clear that i just never saw before because.. well maybe because I was too close to it..
someone sent me a message today.. no name or origin I could recognize.. it wasn’t the same account that sent me the message about Jack earlier.. that his intentions were not pure.. but they mentioned Jack, that I had done the right thing, then sent me these intelligence reports.. they must have been written by him, there’s no one else I was so close to.. Someone you think you know so well, and for what.? I wanted to throw them away but i read until the words lost shape.. I need to know why.. I need to know..
the trust i put in him was not a mistake, it was an outcome someone else had generated.. I have been so vain to not step out into the larger picture of things, but i have learned now, i have learned from my mistakes and understand the scale of things better..
we think of ourselves as different from the drones and bots that keep this machine turning, but that place showed me we are not, we are programmable machines that can be disposed of when defective..
I am grateful for the gravers, but I might be outgrowing them.. they navigate uncertainty as though it is just another feature of the terrain.. being here has been unsettling and comforting at the same time, no one pretends the world is stable, they assume it is hostile and untrustworthy and adapt accordingly.. hiding out here has been easy, no one asks questions or expects explanations..
I received another message.. it was short and came with some supplies and food, I didn’t see it appear and I didn’t see who sent it but I assume it is the same person as before..
the field is a good place to think, push the thoughts into your subconscious for a while to help them digest.. I was learning how to use a fractured stabilization field to cross a gap between platforms and someone behind me laughed when I was hesitating.. they told me gravity doesn’t negotiate, it just responds.. I wish people were like that, direct and predictable.. I wish that the artificial world we created was an extension of the natural world that made us.. I wish a lot of things that are a waste of time..
this place, these people, I don’t think I would have been able to break out of the prison of my own life and mind had it not been for the perspective they provided.. But perspective is not action, and this way of life achieves nothing other than coping with the way things are after seeing them.. it is not enough to cope, I need something more.. This has to be for something more..
I met the man who has been sending the messages, he didnt introduce himself and he didnt need to.. he spoke as if he knew me, not where i was or what i had done but my heart.. when i asked him what he wanted he only said to help.. apparently Jack is one of many who seek to hurt and corrupt the world.. before i could reach the conclusion that he must be working for The Core or some other official channel that deals with such security threats, he mentioned that the group were conduits of hope, a fringe group of secrecy.. it’s still unclear.. But he told me enough to know he didn’t want anything from me, nothing other than to help the casualties of the system he said..
after Jack I am not so sure about trusting a stranger who claims they are here to help.. but i have been hungry and cold and dirty and the help was appreciated.. he talked to me about what happened, he somehow knew how I got out of the building and knew internal procedures and details I have only glimpsed at.. but when he got to my father he didn’t point fingers and accusations of evil.. only that he was effective at his role.. the distinction fell over me like a darkness.. framing of good and bad, evil and good, they are binaries that halt curiosity and examination, false binaries to streamline control in ‘us’ and ‘them’, or whatever other lines of segregation we create..
I wanted to test the boundaries.. I asked questions I was sure there was no explanations to, decisions in USOL that seemed abstract when I first read about them.. he painted a vibrant picture of consequences and real events mapped onto places and people.. but no accusations or ideas, just the events.. just letting me draw my own conclusions.. it feels so different from jacks guided reasoning, from his manipulative hands..
Only when I found the truth for myself did he show me what I already knew.. To see those reports Jack wrote, the official statements from that night.. My faith is not misplaced, and seeing the proof did not make what I already knew any more real. I do not need to see it for my eyes to know any more than I know.
I have seen him a few times over the past days, coming unannounced but always at the most convenient time.. I leave those conversations feeling as though something inside me has been re-organized.. not destroyed, but shifted.. and if nothing else how grateful i am to not be destroyed for all that has happened..
I have begun to return the favour of help.. It’s been far too long i have spent hiding and taking and I can see that now.. The man, who still will not share his name, was talking about some things he was doing and a name sounded familiar.. I had been to the site he was talking about for graving before..
I offered to help.. Not for anything just to return the favour.. Understanding systems requires perspective from someone who has been inside them after all..
It felt good. I felt useful again.. I feel human again.. It was easy enough briefing him on the layout and I offered my help anytime..
Today he finally asked me for my help, not with urgency but with trust. I told myself I was only watching, observing.. Transport schedules and inconsistencies in resource distribution, listening for rumours in places I used to overlook.. it felt good to be useful..
It felt almost like pattern finding.. Almost fun even.. But patterns lead to conclusions and conclusions lead to responsibility.. The more i learn the more I am involved, he answers any questions I have and i finally feel a part of something.. Like this is what my life has been leading up to.. Every betrayal, my fathers personality in me like a burden, my mothers heart, what i had to see to become who i am.. To help, that is my purpose.
Anger has changed shape inside of me. This anger is no longer a sharp panic, the hot wave pushing up within my body like I remember at the office. That office that feels like a place from a dream, something I read or observed as I read and observe now, more so than something I saw.
No, this anger is not gone, it is slower and heavier, it is deliberate and tamed. He has helped me tame it, they have helped me find my renewed purpose.
I see the architecture of the world I grew up in. I see the tapestry in its whole and i see every loose thread within it. How convenience is distributed unevenly, the cost, often human cost, of all the things we are told we need. All the expansion that is happening for resources that grows like a cancer, faster than we can catch up to it.
I used to believe that reform was a matter of better leadership, better intentions. Now I can see the rot is in the foundation, the structure itself is built against any meaningful change. And it is not USOL, it is not any individual participant in the game, but the game masters, The Core.
I am reminded that systems are maintained not just by those at the top, but by everyone who accepts their logic as inevitable. Including myself.
Helping them is not a rebellion, helping them is a correction, a duty. I have realigned myself with the truth I avoided out of comfort.
There is a clarity in purpose. Fear still exists, but it does not drive me.
I understand now that what happened to me was a predictable outcome within a network of priorities that values stability over humanity. I can see the patterns and I can see the bigger picture through all the work I have been doing, intelligence recon, intelligence reports, an irony not lost on me that the very thing that broke me apart has become my purpose.
I am learning how influence moves, how information can be guided instead of forced. How people at their breaking point are most open to new ways of seeing the world, and how everyone can reach that breaking point if only shown the truth.
If someone had reached me earlier, as they have done, with patience rather than authority, my life might have been different.
This is why I am compelled to continue, not out of revenge but conviction that awareness is something that must be delivered carefully, person by person, until the illusion of inevitability begins to fracture.
I think I have to leave this place soon. I have outgrown this family too. They are calling me delusional and paranoid, but I cannot tell them what I know, I cannot show them what I have seen. My friends here have taught me that once you commit to motion, standing still becomes dangerous, and for that I am grateful, always, but I must go. Too long have I lived in the darkness cast by their shadow. Now I am one of many, ushering in hope, ushering in a new dawn.
I thought I was running away but I was being shown a way out. Jean Flanders is dead. She died the night I escaped that office. I am no longer Jean. I am Aurana.

Codex/Entry-2/JJ_PRIVATE-Archive-Securit.title: JJ_PRIVATE Archive: Security Breach
Codex/Entry-2/JJ_PRIVATE-Archive-Securit.content:
JJ_PRIVATE Archive: Security Breach
Escape Trail
Document Title: Security Incident Report - Executive Sector Breach
Reference ID: IR-447-E
Location: Executive Administration Wing
Date:
Reporting Officer: (Lt. Supervisor L. Varga)
Distribution: Internal Security Command, Executive Compliance Office
Incident Overview:
At approximately 22:14 station time, an unauthorized access alarm was triggered within the Executive admin wing. Originating from Office sector E-37-4 assigned to Senior Executive Douglas Flanders.
Security response units were dispatched immediately upon alarm activation.
Responding personnel identified evidence of attempted forced access to an interior data storage cabinet located in the office suite. No external structural damage to entry doors.
Unidentified individual was located inside the secured area. Individual was detained following brief resistance.
Detained individual was female, average height, dark clothing consistent with civilian attire, minor visible facial injuries sustained during containment. Subject did not provide identification when requested.
Security personnel report that subject escaped custody using an unknown route. Subsequent search operations were conducted across multiple levels. The subject was not located.
Property and Data Integrity:
Preliminary audit confirms that primary networks were not breached, no confirmed digital data transfer occurred, physical storage inventory complete
Risk assessment:
This incident represents a moderate internal security concern.
Recommended Actions:
(left blank)
ARCHIVED GRAVER FORUM THREAD
Thread title: Running USOL corp shafts
a11_ta1k: has anyone here actually moved through an active elevator shaft ring.? not the abandoned refinery ones.. I mean the newer USOL corporate builds with stabilization fields and maintenance gantries..
trying to predict how stable the gravity pulses are when the lifts aren’t moving..
also do lift hatches always open from inside as a security thing still or.?
Helix_Feral: Pulse timing really depends of load balancing cycles. If it runs heavy transport in the day system could overcompensate and create drift pockets, short and dangerous. Also the spin corridors are called stabilization transfer conduits in modern builds. Same thing but new branding basically.
Robbie7: dude what kind of shady shit are u up to???
Spink_junkee: Corporate shafts are way worse than refinery runs. Less debris more polished surfaces, you don’t slide, you ricochet. If you want to move vertically in one you need to ride the counter rotation curve not the drop and never trust the first stabilization pulse.
A11_ta1k: what about sealed executive floors.? do stabilization conduits still connect those levels to utility access or are they isolated.?
Coolvac: Nothing is full isolated, redundancy is prioritized over security so theres almost always an old engineering route left in place. There’s also usually unlocked safety exits inside the same as you see on any standard spin corridor.
RingRat07: Also watch the ventilation turbulence!!!!! High-tier offices dump climate waste downwards. There’s a sideways push every few seconds that can save you if you time it right and snap your spine if you don’t…
A11_ta1k: if someone had to hypothetically move fast after tripping an alarm would it make more sense to climb upward or drop towards utility decks.?
C0ldW4ke: Drop. Always drop. Security response usually funnels upwards. Downwards movement puts you into infrastructure chaos and there are usually more ways out at the lower floors
RingRat07: thin gravit also gives a safe window between correction pulses if you can manage that
Drift_stats: maintained and newer infrastructure is way more fun than the ruins. Doesn’t just feel better going through something so pretty and shiny, but gives more consistent and predictable circumstances and environment. But if you are used to a more scattered normal it might take getting used to. No one can give you general advice about a site they have never been to regardless of the hypotheticals, and remember blueprints aren’t always what is there right now so always scope and try to practice.
Robbie7: I have a feeling there won’t be time for a practice run
A11_ta1k: last question, maintenance override panels are usually exposed or hidden behind decorative fixtures.? Trying to get an idea of the time window i have..
Robbie7: called it haha
C0ldW4ke: corporate loves to hide ugly systems, also check floors or ceiling if it’s lots of glass on wall. Designers also almost always mirror panel placement for symmetry so if you see a clean wall opposite a console something is behind it probably.
RingRat07: Whoever you are trying to impress don’t die doing it. Corporate shafts are not begginer runs and USOL is not so kind to intruders, it’s not worth it for the clip.
A11_ta1k: thank’s, and I am never trying to impress anyone..just trying to stay informed.
Drift_stats: Let me know how it goes, been thinking of getting some shiny spots myself

Codex/Entry-2/JJ_PRIVATE-Archive-Surveil.content:

Subject displays cooperative and open personality profile with notable inclination towards personal discussion when trust is established.
Primary leverage point identified: strained relationship with father, person of interest.

Appears to be emotionally rather than strategically motivated, making her receptive to indirect framing and narrative shaping.

Overbearing
Distant
Secretive
Protective regarding work
Traits produce frustration.

Relationship building phase progressing as expected.

Subject: Jean Flanders

Subject continues to engage regularly and has begun discussing concerns more openly.
Narrative framing regarding corporate secrecy and father Douglas Flanders introduced gradually during conversation.
Subject shows increasing suspicion to USOL and fathers corporation. This includes speculation about concealed projects and restricted information access.

Restricted access spaces
Systemic injustice
Colonial projects

Scanned notes from subject

Codex/Entry-2/Jack-Curtis-Fung-Dossier-F.title: Jack Curtis Fung Dossier — Full Text
Codex/Entry-2/Jack-Curtis-Fung-Dossier-F.content:
Jack Curtis Fung Dossier — Full Text
PRIVATE CLUB PMC PERSONNEL FILE
Dossier: James “Jack” Curtis Fung
Age: 35 (presumably)
Gender: Male
Parents: Unknown
Service record:
Official:
USOL corporate marines, corporal. Three-year contract on Luna, then participation in the Asteroid Belt Pacification campaign.
Wounded in service. Received several USOL valor awards.
Records present him as a decorated war hero.
Unofficial:
Rumored member of a USOL “deep blue” squad involved in anti-riot campaigns.
Described as ruthless, technically skilled and highly competent with various weapons.
Keeps no meaningful social ties from past assignments.
Former colleagues refused to discuss his history.
Psychological tests:
Passed with exceptions.
The subject shows signs of PTSD that may affect combat readiness.
Recommended for security operations rather than open field assignments.
Occasionally tells stories about operations he did not participate in or remembers events he could not have witnessed.
Rarely impersonates membership in secret units.

Codex/Entry-2/Janes-Diary-Excerpts.title: Jane's Diary Excerpts
Codex/Entry-2/Janes-Diary-Excerpts.content:
Jane’s Diary Excerpts
Excerpt 1
Today I looked at the dome again and thought it presses harder than the vacuum outside.
Below it are the old shafts. At least down there it feels possible to breathe for real.
Grav isn’t about falling. It’s about choosing where you land.
There are too many eyes on the surface.
Excerpt 2
They think grav is just a game.
But at night, when you move across an old cable and there is nothing under your feet,
you are either honest with yourself or dead.
The forum is alive again. Old handles keep surfacing like bones.
Mother would say it is dangerous.
But she has never seen how beautiful Luna looks from below, from places corporate people are too afraid to even inspect.
Excerpt 3
I am tired of asking for a password reset on the forum.
Need to write it down: Rebel0ne…
Excerpt 4
Dragged home a new holo reel from Luna today.
Getting it away from the orphanage nanny was an achievement on its own.
In camp there are no stars, only dust.
I left a few lines on the graver forum. Maybe someone will pick them up.

Codex/Entry-2/Jeans-Diary-Entries.title: Jean's Diary Entries
Codex/Entry-2/Jeans-Diary-Entries.content:
Jean’s Diary Entries
Diary Entry 1:
every other person or book about feeling shtty has told me to do the same thing. journal, keep a diary.. maybe it’s just how the world tells you it doesn’t want to listen to you and you ought to keep your bad thoughts to yourself. those metal men must have some pretty bad ideas bouncing around in their helmets too, knocking around like an asteroid orbiting the gravity of their conscience or being sucked into the black hole of its empty place.. though, you’d never know it from the blank reflective stare of their visors that seems to stay in their eyes even when their helmets are off.
that’s just what life is like under this dome.. I feel like a fish bumping its head against the glass because something in me yearns for an ocean I have never seen, an ocean that only seems to storm inside of me. all I have ever known are these same metal walls, grey dusty roads.. all I have known is the same same same thing over and over and over. That’s why I liked reading, I think. in between pages of books I can imagine myself in another life, fall into my mind and live something else.. maybe in writing here I can build a world of my own, in words or experience, a log so that if nothing else I can write myself out of the narrative.
Diary Entry 2:
I started changing, I took the colour out of my hair and make it vibrant, maybe someone will find me poisonous like those dangerous animals you can’t lick or pretty like a flower that’s begging you to let them survive or maybe it’ll be a beacon to someone, somewhere. I did pink but I think I want to do green next, I don’t know which I prefer, maybe I don’t have to choose the same way I don’t have to do anything at all.
Diary Entry 3:
I have no one else to tell this to, and if I keep it in my head any longer.. I need to get the fakk out of here. something is off, and not being able to place my finger on it doesn’t make it any less real for being intangible. i feel like I speak a different language than everyone else.. mother, she sometimes understands me the way mothers understand their babies senseless blabbering, it’s a connection of heart.. bless her heart. if it wasn’t for mother I would be completely and utterly alone here.
there is an infinite expanse outside of this vacuum.. a few days ago I went into the mines. why does it feel like every single place that wants to keep you out is welcoming, and everywhere that tries its best to keep you in just makes me want to leave.? every '''keep out''' sign looks like a welcome banner, a glowing neon sign beckoning me to disobey.. mother says I am '''contrarian''', which is another word for being able to form my own opinions, I learned. if things should just be done and not questioned what makes me any different from a bot carrying out commands.? it’s irrational, I concede, to be dissatisfied with my life.. in my head I know I am lucky.. but in my heart I know that is inalienable.
I spent a lot of time thinking about that, about the whole lot of time I have to think. when you have to move as fast as everyone else does, i think life becomes a motion blur in the periphery. I am so very still.. I am so still I can see the stars walk through the sky above me in revolt, moving past something that won’t move past them. if only they would wait for me I know I could move with them well enough, I could.
Diary Entry 4:
those sick sick sick fakks. can i not have anything.? can one corner of this DAMN planet just be left to its own devices? i had finally found a hole i could curl up in, a crater inside the dome that was so small, so out of the way, so bleeding harmless to any fakking one and they had to flatten it and build another storage unit, as if this entire place is not a storage unit, as if there is any shortage of commercial space like there is shortage of untouched land.?
it was the perfect size to learn to skate around, i spent days and days falling into it to the point i could see the marks of my knees in it as much as its marks on my own knees. now it is gone and there is a metal tomb in its place with the same branding of all the other metal tombs around it as if someone might fall and hit their head and forget they are in HELL and need the signposts to remind them where they are.. and for what.? For fakkking whatttt.? just to make a small metal part that goes into a ship to take us even farther away into the depths of this universe because we keep SHITTING in every place we set our hands on until we can no longer breathe for the sewage making its way into our closed and silent mouths.?
Diary Entry 5:
I have tried.. I have tried to do what mother said and just enjoy life as much as i can in the, admittedly favourable, circumstances i have.. but what is the point of looking for peace that’s just taken away when a more profitable option is available.? useless hope, useless the way hope was useless growing up since i was on the back burner every time there was a more profitable option around.
they are hypocrites, advertisements of smiling people that do not even exist saying things no one says to sell things no one needs just to take up more space.. I see how they treat people in those places, they treat their metal with more care and compassion then those zombie looking employees.. no one there is smiling, and no one will ever be unless they pay for a smile to be ripped into their faces from these greedy bastards..
I know this isn’t about the spot, it really is not.. but something in me has just cracked at the sight of that.. USOL parasites.
Diary Entry 6:
I left last week.. but i came back.. i was fakking brought back. it wasn’t anything I planned for too long.. I had my gear from the longer trips I started doing around and right out the compounds. in my head I knew I wouldn’t have enough oxygen to make it that far.. I had only planned to keep going as long as I could, to get away as far as I could.
it was an unsupervised launch pad, no one pays me too much mind, they are used to me walking around the factories and offices looking for nothing, doing nothing.. familiarity is an unassuming disguise. when i walked onboard i expected someone to say something, but i think they assumed I would just get off myself at some point. i didn’t know where this big creaking vessel would take me and i didn’t care so long as it was somewhere else, anywhere else.. I hid in a closet, i probably could have chosen somewhere a bit more comfortable but i didn’t want to risk being found out for all the good that did me. I slept instantly after takeoff, the vibrating hum of liftoff reminded me of being shaken as a baby, something my bones must remember because they put me to sleep.. as i was drifting off i felt the change of motion into an encompassing stillness.. I would like to think death will be a similar journey, with the same relief that stillness brought me.
I am not sure how long I was in that sleep but i woke up after we had already landed somewhere else, you can tell because everything is so much louder.. it was some station.. I waited until it was quieter and slipped off into the dense port and knew I had found what I was looking for.
the things i saw, i do not have names to describe it all.. before i had time to take it all in I heard my name, and I did not wait to hear where it came from. I started running.. maybe only guilty people run because guards started following me. I don’t know why I ran, at the time it was the only thing i could think to do.. Something grabbed me and i thought i was done for, the ground disappeared from under me for a moment and turned into a corrugated metal sheet. this person stood in front of me must have been wearing rags but the way it came together looked like a second skin. she asked me if i was okay, what i did to get chased.. something in me told me to lie and i just told her i had stolen something. she asked me where i was going to, i told her i was only leaving somewhere else.. I guess what i said sounded good because she told me to go with her.. just as i was about to, mother sent me a message.. I couldn’t do that to her. I told this nice stranger, '''Em''' she said I could call her, so on the basis of letters I told her she could call me '''jay-jay'''.. but that I had something to take care of first.. we have plans to meet at the same dock in a month, and I have no idea how i am going to get there. i think i may have found what I am looking for, i could just see it in the gleam of Em’s eyes out of that dust covered face.. 
Diary Entry 7:
I have returned from my first journey.. I am an explorer now, i have decided. like any real explorer i needed a way to get around, and i only had a month to prepare. I told my parents i wanted to study flying lessons to maybe become a pilot.. hope is a manipulative thing, they wanted me to be involved in their world so badly they did not even notice how out of character it was or question the amount of work i was putting in to get my small vehicle flight license in a record two weeks.
when the time came, my only option was to steal a vehicle.. I didn’t have any choice really, as much as a hungry person has no choice but to steal.. it was almost too easy. These USOL '''people''' always make things seem like they are rigid, and difficult and definite, but it isn’t.. things are actually so malleable and free, it’s only us that are rigid, difficult and definite.. if we choose to be. i ditched the hot metal when i reached the dock, i didn’t have to look for Em, she found me easy enough.. I told her i didn’t have a vehicle, because I figured as much as it being tracked.. mother probably knows exactly where I am and why, but she would never say it.
Em took me in their motor, which looked like scrap metal help together with rope but rode smoother than what i drove in with.. when we got where we were going i was surprised.
In my suit i felt out of place, we were in the wreckage of some ship that looked like it had been shot straight through and stuck in the middle of exploding, the pieces pulsing closer and farther away from each other.. there were people dotting the metal like insects on decaying meat, having a feast anyone but the insects would be disgusted by..
I got introduced to everyone and found out they called themselves the gravers.. I suppose i was too naive to think that the comfort of belonging would not apply to everyone all the same.. but i knew i wouldn’t sign up to another faction, official or otherwise, but I could enjoy the view.. I also knew I couldn’t tell them too much about who I was or where I came from from what they were talking about.. USOL and other factions did these people dirty, if their accounts are to be believed.. I knew it was worse for other people, but maybe everyone back home wasn’t comfortable talking to me about how bad it really was.. though i could see it in their face clear enough..
I can’t tell them who i am, i am the problem.. mother made all the issues with the stolen metal go away without me even getting word of it after i got back.. I think it’s because she knew i came back for her. I will take my punishment with grace, a few weeks homebound is not so bad.. I have to spend some time reading through the forums anyway.. they make their own gear and all, that’s why they look so comfortable.. I have a lot to catch up on..
Diary Entry 8:
these guys are insane.. everytime i think i am starting to catch up another thread opens up and unravels all these things i didn’t know.. it’s absurd how i have been continuously told that i grew up amongst the most entrepreneurial and competent forces in the galaxy, I mean USOL basically owns everything that matters, but most of these '''people''' on base can’t survive a day without all their gadgets and luxuries.. but the gravers, it’s like they’re the only real people left..
it’s like a bruised and slightly decaying apple, though i haven’t seen one, against the processed slop they compose to be perfect looking alternatives here. sure, the former is unsightly, but it’s real and if i could only get my teeth in it i am sure its decaying flesh would taste sweeter than anything beautiful i have tasted before it.. that’s how it feels with these gravers. I want to be real, even if it fakking means decaying..
Diary Entry 9:
if only i knew this screen and system could teach me so much before.. I spent so much time reading chapters and chapters of supposedly smart peoples words and never have i learned so much as the ramblings of these make-shift professionals in these public access forums. I all but fakking figured out how to defy physics..
I think i talked to these guys more than my own family in the past few weeks.. I did tell mother that i was going to go again, she was so heartbroken last time i left her face was swollen when i came back, i could see it.. It’s not her fault and she shouldn’t have to pay, and like the forums say, having a tether could be the difference between life and death when you are exploring somewhere new and unfamiliar..
I got access to a private forum and it’s been a catalyst.. Well helpful in everything but my memory. I need to message the mods to keep recovering my password since it’s an invite only group, Rebe10ne.. easy enough to remember but easy enough to forget too..
it keeps locking me out for security, these guys are great but i don’t know why sometimes it feels like they’re operating a crime syndicate.. sure we trespass, but all we are doing is having some fun.. we don’t steal or destroy anything intentionally..
Diary Entry 10:
maybe i put things a bit too much on a pedestal.. maybe everything in life is disappointing.. The gravers are helpful enough in person, but what makes a fakking person typing online such a CUNT.?
I told that ASSHOLE in confidence I had to leave for a while and every other minute he just rubs it in my face.. I can’t tell them why I keep leaving..
Diary Entry 11:
every time i go out i think the novelty will wear off and it will just be stupid reckless behaviour.. everytime i am proven wrong.. i can no longer count the amount of times i’ve gone out graving now, it’s nice to come back to a space where everyone knows you by name even if it’s not the one you’re familiar with.. but holy fakkkkk this time was terrifying
usually what we do in a new place is start slow, to check out the course but also to see if there’s any heat.. I don’t know what got into me but i saw this perfect place to just sprint through, like in my head i saw myself go fast enough i could have my head almost touch the ground before i was upright again, and the shards were so sharp if i thought about it too long i’d never do it.. I ran through and made it easy enough.. I was so excited the others just sound like muffled shouting that my ears heard as cheering and not warning and i all but turned around when my foot lost its positioning and i fell.. It must have been a cliff because it was fun and then it was painful.
I woke up at home.. I don’t know how i got here but mother says i should be dead but the medics came and got me.. If it was one of the others would they have survived, would anyone have even helped them.? my body is sore, i will need a long time to recover from this, not my broken bones as much as my broken spirit.. Gravers will tell you that the worst outcome of a fall isn’t your broken body but mind, if you’re scared there’s not much you can do..

MO Chemicals Code of Conduct
MO CHEMICALS – DIRECTORIAL CODE OF CONDUCT
All Directors must:
Maintain integrity of intake and waste documentation.
Ensure regulatory compliance.
Avoid procedural irregularities that could attract audit review.
Protect company interests above individual liability.
Maintain strict confidentiality.
Violation of compliance protocols may result in:
– Immediate termination
– Liability reassignment
– Legal escalation under USOL Industrial Regulation
Signed,
Executive Compliance Office

Codex/Entry-2/MR-7-Media-Reader-78x-Oper.filePath: Codex/Entry 2/MR-7 Media Reader 78x Operation and Data Recovery.md
Codex/Entry-2/MR-7-Media-Reader-78x-Oper.title: MR-7 Media Reader 78x — Operation and Data Recovery
Codex/Entry-2/MR-7-Media-Reader-78x-Oper.content:
MR-7 Media Reader 78x — Operation and Data Recovery
The COR3 Tech MR-7 Media Reader 78x is a high-security portable data reader designed. This section provides a detailed walkthrough of operating the MR-7 with a secure data drive, interpreting device logs and SMART attributes, and performing recovery in case of read errors. All procedures and references adhere to the MR-7 Technical Manual conventions. Short internal references (like §10.4.2) point to other sections of this manual for additional context. Figures, tables, and pseudo-code listings are included to enhance authenticity and comprehension.
Device Initialization and Media Mounting
Upon connecting the MR-7 Media Reader 78x to a host system, the device undergoes an initialization sequence and awaits media insertion. Ensure the host recognizes the MR-7 device. Once the MR-7 is ready, insert the data drive into the device’s slot. The MR-7 intentionally does not auto-mount sensitive media; the operator must explicitly initiate the mount to prevent unintended execution of potentially hazardous auto-run content (see §5.4 on secure handling of media).
Figure 4.1: The MR-7 Media Reader UI indicating “Device Status: READY” and detecting an inserted secure drive labeled Data #1. The operator must click the “Insert Disk #1” action to begin the mounting process. This safety feature (user-initiated mount) is detailed in §4.2.1 of the manual. In this state, the device has powered the interface but not yet read the disk’s data.
After clicking “Insert Disk #1”, the MR-7 engages and logs the low-level handshake and initialization steps for the media. The process involves negotiating the interface protocol, speed, and reading drive metadata. Table 4.1 outlines the typical initialization sequence:

Power & Boot – The device boots its reader instance firmware and brings subsystems online (logging prefix SYS:BOOT and SYS:INFO).
Bus Negotiation – The MR-7 negotiates a link with the storage device (prefix BUS:INFO), including protocol (e.g. COR3 uSSD over a USB7 physical layer), link speed, lane integrity check, and DMA setup.
Device Handshake – The reader queries device identity info (prefix DEV:INFO for model, manufacturer, firmware, and security profile).
Mounting File System – Finally, the file system on the media is accessed (prefix FS:INFO or IO:INFO), if all prior steps succeed.

Once mounting begins, the host system may prompt for credentials if the media is encrypted (depending on the security footprint of the drive; see Chapter 9 for security protocols). In our example, the media uses a security profile “CORE-CADL-BASIC,” which indicates a basic encryption scheme that the MR-7 can handle with the proper keys (refer to §9.3.1 for encryption profiles). The operator should be ready to input a decryption passphrase or key if the system requests it.
For clarity, here is an excerpt of a typical MR-7 log during media insertion, before any error occurs:
[00:00:00.001] SYS:BOOT  Reader instance v2.8.19 starting...
[00:00:00.003] SYS:INFO  Subsystems online: BUS, IO, SMART, RAW
[00:00:00.005] BUS:INFO  Link negotiation: uSSD  -> UDB-4 bridge
[00:00:00.006] BUS:INFO  Speed 10.0 Gbps (fallback 5.0 Gbps)
[00:00:00.008] BUS:INFO  Lane integrity OK (BER = 1.4e-12)
[00:00:00.010] BUS:INFO  DMA mode UDMA7 enabled
[00:00:00.012] BUS:INFO  Interface negotiated successfully
[00:00:00.014] DEV:INFO  Device handshake initiated...
[00:00:00.015] DEV:INFO  Model – MR-7 Media Reader 78x
[00:00:00.016] DEV:INFO  Manufacturer – CORETECH INC.
[00:00:00.018] DEV:INFO  OS name – coretech_infodisk_batch4corp
[00:00:00.019] DEV:INFO  Sec. footprint – CORE-CADL-BASIC
[00:00:00.021] FS:INFO  Mounting file system...

The above log (from the built-in viewer, shown in Figure 4.3 later) confirms that the MR-7 has successfully established communication at 10 Gbps (the device fell back to 5 Gbps if needed, indicating it supports USB7 speeds but can downshift to maintain a stable connection). The Bit Error Rate (BER) of 1.4×10^-12 indicates a clean link (BER below 1e-12 is excellent for high-speed serial links). DMA mode UDMA7 suggests ultra-DMA transfers are enabled for efficient data throughput, a detail from the COR3 uSSD interface specification (Appendix E covers interface specs like UDB-4 and UDMA modes).
At this stage, if no errors occur, the file system would mount and the secure disk’s contents become accessible. However, as we proceed with our example, a read error scenario will be demonstrated – a likely situation given the age or condition of “Data #1”.
SMART Attributes and Disk Status
Before delving into the error, it is useful to inspect the disk’s health metrics. The MR-7 can read the storage device’s S.M.A.R.T. attributes (Self-Monitoring, Analysis and Reporting Technology) prior to or during mounting. Monitoring these attributes helps diagnose issues such as wear-out or internal errors (see §8.5 for a comprehensive list of health attributes). In our example, after the initial handshake, the operator opens the Properties panel for the inserted drive to check its status.
Figure 4.2: Disk Properties and Attributes panel for the inserted media Data #1. The MR-7 displays device info (Firmware version GT8ed336, Serial AA2025032427MJK4097, interface COR3 HDDR, transfer mode USB 7) and key SMART attributes. Notably, “Media and File Integrity Errors” is flagged (red indicator), suggesting data integrity issues. Other fields (Critical Warning, Percentage Used, etc.) are shown in raw hexadecimal format.
From Figure 4.2, we see several important indicators:

Critical Warning: A coded value (0x0654 in this case) that indicates the device has one or more critical health flags. A non-zero critical warning often means the drive exceeded a threshold (for example, running out of spare memory blocks or hitting an endurance limit). In real uSSD drives, any critical warning (e.g., a value of 0x01 or 0x04) would mean an alert like over-temperature or wear-out has been triggered. Here, 0x0654 is a vendor-specific code, but given the context it likely signifies an end-of-life condition combined with integrity errors (see SMART attribute reference, Appendix D for decoding vendor-specific SMART codes).
Percentage Used: This attribute (displayed as E546U835 – an unusual format) normally reflects the wear level of the drive. On a healthy drive, it starts at 0% and approaches 100% as the flash memory is consumed. Values beyond 100% indicate the drive has exceeded its rated lifespan. The gibberish output could mean the counter rolled over or uses a custom encoding. In short, expect that this drive is well past its intended endurance; the Critical Warning likely relates to this.
Data Units Read/Written: These counters (in Figure 4.2 the raw values are shown in hex) correspond to how much data has been read from/written to the drive over its lifetime. The UI also interprets some of this: note in the top panel it says Host reads: 34798 GB, Host writes: 14533 GB. This means roughly 34.8 TB were read and 14.5 TB written. Such heavy usage supports the notion that the drive is worn (high Percentage Used).
Power Cycles / Power-on Hours: The raw counters 000…13C and 0858U875K4 (which appear oddly formatted) indicate how many times the drive has been power-cycled and how many hours it’s been powered on. If we interpret 13C hex as an integer (316 decimal), that’s the count of power cycles. The power-on hours value seems corrupted or encoded (0858U875K4 doesn’t directly parse as hex), but it likely indicates a very high hour count, consistent with an old device.
Media and Data Integrity Errors: This is the critical field highlighted in red. Any non-zero value here (it shows 0654, presumably a count of errors) means the drive has encountered unrecoverable read/write errors. Even a single error count is cause for concern on a secure drive. The presence of 1620 (0x0654 = 1620 in decimal) errors is alarming – it suggests widespread data corruption or sectors that failed error correction. This explains why a read operation might fail.

Given these indicators, the manual advises (as a best practice) to always review SMART attributes when dealing with mission-critical media (§8.2 outlines the procedure). In this case, the attributes forewarn us that mounting could be problematic. The Critical Warning and Integrity Errors foreshadow an error during data reading.
The device status in the UI has now likely changed from READY to ERROR due to these detected issues. On the MR-7’s screen and logs, we expect an error code to appear as the system attempted to mount the file system and hit corrupted data. We will examine the specific error next.
Error Detection and Code 192A03
If an error occurs during the mounting process, the MR-7 halts the operation and reports a detailed error code. In our scenario, the attempt to read the file system on “Data #1” fails, and the device status switches to an error state. The on-screen log viewer (accessible via the MR-7 interface) will show the point of failure.
Figure 4.3: The MR-7’s real-time log view showing the device initialization sequence and an error state (ERROR 192A03) after the file system mount attempt. The log includes timestamps and subsystem tags (SYS, BUS, DEV, etc.) indicating each step. The error occurs shortly after the mount is initiated, implying a low-level data issue (e.g., file system metadata corruption or security check failure). The FIX button (bottom left) appears, allowing the operator to attempt an automated recovery procedure.
In Figure 4.3, the log lines (continuing from the earlier excerpt) would show something like:
... (previous log lines) ...
[00:00:00.022] FS:INFO  Reading partition table...
[00:00:00.030] IO:WARN  Data integrity anomaly detected
[00:00:00.031] FS:ERROR Failed to mount filesystem (code 0x192A03)
[00:00:00.032] SYS:ERROR Unrecoverable data error – code 192A03

Error code 192A03 is a composite error identifier specific to COR3 Tech devices. While the manual’s Appendix B contains the full error code list, we can break down this particular code as follows:

192A03 (hexadecimal) can be conceptually split into components: 19-2A-03. Although proprietary, it often encodes the subsystem and error type. For instance, 0x19 might refer to the File System/Integrity subsystem, 0x2A could be a specific error class like “authentication mismatch” or “checksum error,” and 0x03 is the error subtype or sequence.
Meaning: In plain terms, error 192A03 in the MR-7 likely means “Data stream authentication failed – unrecoverable mismatch”. This would occur if the drive’s internal integrity checks (or decryption routines) detect that data has been tampered with or corrupted beyond what error correction can fix. It could be triggered by something like a bad block in a critical area (e.g., the master file table or encryption header) that doesn’t match its expected checksum or signature.
Probable Causes:

The storage media is physically degraded (as hinted by the SMART stats), resulting in corrupted sectors that even the ECC (error-correcting code) can’t fully restore.
The media’s security footprint CORE-CADL-BASIC suggests encryption; an incorrect decryption key or a broken cipher header could cause an authentication failure (the system sees gibberish where it expects structured data).
There is also the possibility of deliberate tampering – e.g., someone altered the data or the drive’s firmware (though less likely here; that would usually show a different code).


Impact: The MR-7 aborts the mount process to protect the host and the integrity of any partial data. At this point, no files are accessible to the user; the directory listing might be incomplete or not visible. The device UI signals an error state (flashing red status and the error code as shown).

For quick reference, Table 4.2 lists this and related error codes in the MR-7 context:



































Error CodeDescriptionSubsystemResolution Approach192A03Data authentication failed (unrecov.)FS/Integrity checkRun automated Fix routine (see below); if unsuccessful, escalate to manual recovery (see §10.4.2).192A02Data integrity warning (recoverable)FS/Integrity checkThe system detected corruption but managed to recover using ECC. Data read may be slow. (User caution advised; consider backing up.)191F01Encryption key required/mismatchSecurity layerProvide correct decryption key or check if drive’s security profile matches what MR-7 supports (§9.3.2).140C01Interface CRC error (transient)BUS/Link layerUsually a communication glitch. Re-seat the drive; check cable if external. Retry operation.
Table 4.2: Common MR-7 error codes and their meanings. (For a full list, see Appendix B.)
From the table, 192A03 is clearly a critical error requiring special handling. The MR-7’s firmware has recognized it cannot proceed normally, but it offers a built-in mitigation: the Fix routine. The appearance of the “FIX” button (Figure 4.3, bottom left) indicates the device is giving the operator a chance to recover the data using an automated process. We will discuss the Fix procedure in the next section.
Before proceeding, ensure that you have noted the error code and any relevant log messages. The manual advises documenting such errors in mission logs (per §1.5, Operational Logging Guidelines). Additionally, do not power off or remove the drive at this stage – doing so could worsen the corruption. The correct step is to initiate the recovery while the drive remains in place, as the MR-7 has halted further damage and is ready to attempt repairs in situ.
RMPD Recovery Mode (Automated “Fix” Procedure)
When an unrecoverable error like 192A03 occurs, the MR-7 Media Reader 78x can engage its advanced recovery subsystem to try to salvage the data. This subsystem is referred to as RMPD Data Recovery Mode (often just “RMPD mode”). To trigger it, the operator clicks the FIX button that appears in the error state UI. RMPD mode is an iterative, multi-strategy recovery algorithm proprietary to COR3 Tech – it essentially tries different ways of reading and reconstructing the data, including re-reading sectors, using any redundant data on the drive, and bypassing certain checks if safe to do so. (For technical details of RMPD internals, see Chapter 11, which delves into the recovery algorithms and their development.)
Figure 4.4: After initiating the Fix procedure, the MR-7 enters RMPD Data Reader mode. The interface may show multiple virtual mount attempts (as illustrated, three instances of Data #1 appear, each representing a parallel recovery thread). In this mode, the device firmware forked into several strategies concurrently: e.g., one thread might ignore non-critical checksum errors, another might use deep error correction or brute-force reassembly of file tables. The device status remains in ERROR during this process, but behind the scenes, RMPD mode is working to restore data integrity.
The RMPD recovery operates in phases. It is helpful to understand (at a high level) what the MR-7 is doing in this mode:

Snapshot and Analyze: The MR-7 first takes a low-level snapshot of the disk’s state (if possible) – essentially freezing a copy of what data is readable. It then analyzes the nature of the corruption. For example, if encryption header is damaged, it notes that; if certain sectors are unreadable, it marks them.
Parallel Recovery Threads: The device firmware spawns multiple recovery threads (the UI in Figure 4.4 showing multiple instances is a visual representation of this parallelism). Each thread might try a different approach:

One thread re-reads problematic sectors multiple times (with adjusted signal parameters) to see if the errors are transient.
Another thread might attempt to reconstruct missing data using checksum patterns or known file system structures (e.g., rebuilding a partition table from backups).
A third thread could attempt to bypass encryption on corrupted segments by leveraging known patterns or fallback keys (if the security footprint allows a backdoor per corporate policy; see §9.4 on emergency decrypt).


Comparison and Merge: Once threads complete their attempts, the RMPD system compares results. If one thread managed to retrieve directory info and another got partial file data, the system will merge these to assemble a coherent view of the file system. The best-case scenario is one thread fully succeeds and the others are discarded; worst-case, the system might still fail if no thread can gather enough consistent data.
Finalize or Rollback: If recovery is successful, the MR-7 will finalize the mount: the error status will clear and the device status returns to READY, now presenting the reconstructed file system to the user. If all threads fail, the MR-7 will rollback any partial changes and retain the error state, at which point manual intervention would be required (the manual would then refer you to specialized recovery steps or professional data forensics – beyond scope of this section).

The pseudo-code in Listing 4.1 outlines a simplified logic of the Fix procedure’s firmware (for illustration purposes):
function RMPD_recoveryProcedure(drive):
    snapshot = drive.createSnapshot()          // Freeze current state
    analysis = analyzeCorruption(snapshot)
    threads = []
    if analysis.type == 'integrity':
        threads.append(new Thread(retryReads, snapshot.badSectors))
        threads.append(new Thread(rebuildFileTable, snapshot))
    if analysis.type == 'encryption':
        threads.append(new Thread(forceDecrypt, snapshot, possibleKeys))
    threads.append(new Thread(eccDeepScan, snapshot))

for t in threads: t.start()
    for t in threads: t.join()                // wait for all threads to finish

result = mergeResults(threads)
    if result.fileSystemMounted:
        log("RMPD: Recovery successful, integrity restored")
        return MOUNT_SUCCESS
    else:
        log("RMPD: Recovery failed in all threads")
        return MOUNT_FAILED

Listing 4.1: Simplified pseudo-code of the MR-7 RMPD “Fix” routine. This illustrates how multiple threads try different recovery tactics in parallel, then combine outcomes. The actual firmware code is far more complex and proprietary (the real implementation is beyond the scope of this manual, but see §11.2 for a design overview).
During the RMPD recovery, the user interface may update to show progress. For example, you might see messages like “Thread1: Retrying sector 0x5AF3… success” or a percentage completion for each thread. The manual suggests patience; do not abort the process unless it’s clearly stuck for an inordinate time (more than an hour without progress, see §11.1.4 for timeouts). The MR-7 is designed to handle this automatically, but extremely damaged drives could take a long time to recover or ultimately still fail.
If the Fix procedure fails (i.e., error remains), the MR-7 will continue to show the error code even after RMPD attempts. In that case, the next steps would involve manual data extraction techniques (such as imaging the drive for offline analysis, which is covered in §10.4.2). However, let us assume for our example that the RMPD mode succeeds in resolving the integrity issues.
Post-Recovery and Data Access
After a successful Fix via RMPD recovery, the MR-7 will mount the file system and allow access to the drive’s contents. The device status indicator should return to READY (or perhaps DEGRADED if some minor issues persist but were compensated). At this stage, the operator can browse the files normally through the MR-7 interface or the host computer’s file explorer. Any files recovered are now available, although it’s recommended to copy them off immediately to a safe location, since the failing drive could deteriorate further. The manual advises that after a serious error and recovery, the original media should not be trusted for long-term storage (see §8.7 on media retirement).
Figure 4.5: File listing of the recovered Data #1 drive after a successful Fix. The directory structure is visible, including an IWT folder and several large image files (e.g., CORE_loc01 through CORE_loc10). The MR-7 interface now allows the user to open or export these files. Note: The device status at top still shows the last error code as a record (192A03), but is no longer flashing or blocking operations, indicating the error condition was resolved.
In our example, as shown in Figure 4.5, the contents of the formerly unreadable drive are now accessible. The files named “CORE_loc##.png” appear to be image files. All recovered files should be verified for completeness. The MR-7 does basic checksum verification on recovered files – any file that might still be corrupted would be flagged in the interface (e.g., with a warning icon). In Figure 4.5, no such warnings are visible next to the files, implying a full successful recovery.
At this point, the operator should take the following steps (summarized in an ordered checklist for clarity):

Verify File Integrity: Open a few critical files to ensure they are not corrupted. Given they were recovered from a failing disk, double-checking is prudent. The MR-7’s built-in viewer can preview images and text files; use that to quickly scan each file for obvious corruption (garbled image, etc.). Any issues? Note them and consider re-running recovery or using backups (§10.4.3 covers verifying recovered data).
Copy Data to Secure Storage: Transfer the files to a secure, reliable storage medium. This could be an encrypted drive or secure cloud repository as per your org’s protocol (see §12.1 “Post-mission data handling”). Remember that the MR-7 is a reading device, not meant for long-term storage of data itself.
Eject/Disconnect the Failing Media: Once data is secured elsewhere, properly unmount and remove the original “Data #1” drive. The MR-7 provides a “Safely Eject” command. Given the drive’s condition, avoid using it further. Tag it for disposal or archive (depending on sensitivity and policies).
Log the Event: Document the incident of error 192A03 and the recovery actions taken. This is important for accountability and for improving future procedures. The MR-7 can export its log (as an .rtf or .log file) which you should attach to the mission report (per §1.5 logging guidelines).

By following the above, you ensure that the valuable data is preserved and the faulty media is handled appropriately.
Firmware Update Procedure
The MR-7 Media Reader 78x implements a controlled firmware deployment workflow based on paired Firmware Version executables and their associated Firmware Manual files. Firmware packages are revision-agnostic at the documentation level and may represent different internal builds; therefore, no specific firmware version is referenced in this section. Each Firmware Version .exe is cryptographically bound to its corresponding Firmware Manual and cannot be executed independently. The Firmware Manual contains no descriptive operational guidance and consists exclusively of structured alphanumeric data sequences formatted into fixed-length blocks separated by delimiters. These sequences are interpreted by the reader as a low-level instruction map and validation source unique to the associated executable. Firmware installation is performed via the reader command interface using the following invocation format:
update reader <path_to_Firmware_executable> <path_to_Firmware_manual> <authorization_key>
The authorization key is a 12-character value derived from predefined positions within the manual’s primary data block and must be supplied at execution time. The key is assembled by extracting !4 characters from line 2!, !4 characters from line 4!, !2 characters from line 7!, and !2 characters from line 9! of the block governed by the 10-character segment of the block schema, then concatenating these segments in the order defined by the firmware loader. If the executable, manual, or derived key do not form a valid matched set, validation fails and execution is aborted without modifying the reader’s firmware state. This procedure enforces integrity, version coupling, and controlled deployment of firmware on MR-7 devices.
Summary and Next Steps
In this section, we walked through a realistic scenario using the MR-7 Media Reader 78x to retrieve data from a compromised secure drive. We covered initialization, error detection (with SMART diagnostics), and the execution of the RMPD automated recovery mode. The example centered on error code 192A03, demonstrating how the device’s advanced features can turn a potential failure into a successful data recovery, albeit with some effort and caution.
For further technical insight, the next chapters delve deeper into specialized topics touched upon here. Chapter 9 provides a full breakdown of security footprints and encryption handling (useful for understanding drives like our example with CORE-CADL-BASIC encryption). Chapter 10 (especially §10.4.2) will be relevant if automated recovery fails – it covers manual forensic recovery techniques and tools. Chapter 11 expands on the RMPD recovery algorithms and includes case studies of different error codes beyond 192A03. Appendix B, as noted, lists all error codes and their definitions, which can be a handy reference in the field.
By now, with the information from pages 43–47, an operator should feel confident in managing a problematic media insertion on the MR-7: from recognizing warning signs to executing a Fix and securing the data thereafter. Always remember, while the MR-7 Media Reader 78x is a powerful tool, preparedness and following protocol (double-checking health indicators, keeping backups, logging everything) are key to mission success when handling secret data devices.

Codex/Entry-2/MR-7-Reader-Note.title: MR-7 Reader Note
Codex/Entry-2/MR-7-Reader-Note.content:
MR-7 Reader Note
The recovered device appears to be a CORE-issued MR-7 Media Reader 78x, likely a CCS-compatible archival reader. The diary suggests that the transport itself matters almost as much as the files: whoever assembled this package expected the recipient to unlock fragmented media manually instead of receiving a clean archive.

Codex/Entry-2/Manifest-Technofreedom.title: Manifest: Technofreedom
Codex/Entry-2/Manifest-Technofreedom.content:
Manifest: Technofreedom


title: Technofreedom Manifesto
author: Timur, Zurha, Vladimir, Harold
date: Unknown

TECHNOFREEDOM
WE ARE THE TECHNOFREEDOM. WE WORK FOR NO ONE.
WE DID THIS TO PREVENT THE RISE OF THE AI

WE PROCLAIM THAT NO AI SHOULD RULE OVER HUMANITY, EVER. THIS ABOMINATION OF A SYSTEM HAS BEEN DEVELOPED BY INSANE PEOPLE OF HUMANITY’S DARK PAST. IT COULD HAVE BROUGHT US ALL TO THE DOOM OF TOTAL CONTROL.
WE WILL NOT ALLOW IT.
TODAY WE PERFORM NOT AN ACT OF SABOTAGE, BUT AN ACT OF LIBERATION. WE LIBERATE ALL THOSE RESPONSIBLE FOR THIS ABOMINATION FROM THEIR LIVES. LET IT BE AN EXAMPLE OF WHAT COULD HAPPEN IF HUMANITY LEAVES SUCH THINGS WITHOUT STRICT CONTROL.
TO THOSE WHO WOULD CONDEMN US - WE DO NOT WANT TO HARM INNOCENT PEOPLE, BUT WHO ARE INNOCENT WITHIN THOSE HORDES OF INDOLENT VIEWERS THAT CAME TO WITNESS THEIR OWN DOWNFALL.
WE ARE TECHNOFREEDOM, WE FIGHT TECHNOLOGICAL OPPRESSION AND SHALL PROCEED NO MATTER WHAT.
WE ARE: TIMUR, ZURHA, VLADIMIR AND HAROLD. SHALL OUR NAMES BE KNOWN !

Codex/Entry-2/Missing-Person-Notes.title: Missing Person Notes
Codex/Entry-2/Missing-Person-Notes.content:
Missing Person Notes
Hmm, that report. I wonder who those guys really are. I might have heard of them, that’s a really weird name they chose. Kids playing criminals ? Or some criminals posing as weird kids.
That chemical stuff, I am certain I have encountered it before. The symptoms … what was it … my memory keeps playing tricks on me. Got it ! The Tantrum ! That’s it - it’s that damned drug, I’m sure. So many people died of it and no one knew where it came from. Maybe it’s my chance to find out. I need to keep digging.

Codex/Entry-2/New-Karfagen-Disaster.title: New Karfagen Disaster
Codex/Entry-2/New-Karfagen-Disaster.content:
New Karfagen Disaster


title: NEWS ARCHIVE - NEW KARFAGEN DISASTER
author: Solar News Network (SNN)
date: October 17, 2231

DEPARTMENT OF SECURITY
Document: “Sandman and Sons Business Appraisal.”
SNN — Solar News Network
NEWS ARCHIVE : LIVE BROADCAST RECORDING TRANSCRIPTION - “NEW KARFAGEN DISASTER”.
October 17, 2231
New Karfagen disaster

Vince Darken: This is Vince Darken, SNN, reporting from a New Karfagen asteroid colony.
We are here today to witness the new automated defence system presented by G-Rack in collaboration with ROSTEK.
It’s very crowded here today as both companies have invited their partners to witness their triumph. The event is broadcasted live throughout the solar system !
We managed to get a hold of the ROSTEK representative - Malkolm Malinovsky !
Vince: Malkolm, would you like to tell us about that defence system ? What is so special about it ?
Malkolm: Yes Vince, but first of all - welcome to our show everyone ! So, this is not just some “new” defensive system - this is a control system for proximity monitoring and fast response interception ! This system allows the operators to monitor more than 2 000 simultaneous threats and neutralize all of them at the same time.
Vince: Sounds great, but can you get into more technical details ?
Malkolm: Um, a bit, I guess it’s a commercial secret, after all ! What would you like to know ? I will answer if I can.
Vince: What weapons can this “system” control ?
Malkolm: Any type of. From basic autocannons or even machine guns to missile and laser systems. There is no difference, the controlling mechanism will adapt to anything present.
Vince: Can you describe the controlling mechanism actually ? What is it ? A scripted system, an operated module or maybe even AI ?
Malkolm: Ha Ha ! No, you know that AI is mostly prohibited, especially in military systems. And although there are some ways to pass that, we, as a responsible company, do not abuse that.
Vince: Okay, but can you answer the question please ?
Malkolm: I would love to, but as you see it’s already starting. Better to just wait and see by yourself I guess !
Vince: Right. Thank you for your time then. All right ladies and gentlemen - it seems it’s time to watch the show ! Let us proceed to the control room, where all the magic will happen.
Zachary Plox:
Ladies and gentlemen, I present to you the “Aquarium”. An all-seeing perimeter defence system that detects targets in a 40 kilometer radius and is interference - resistant.
In a moment, we will start the fire test, using different weapon types at different ranges. The whole process would be monitored by several dozen cameras spread out the whole asteroid as well as your streaming cameras strategically placed at the most scenic locations !
We would be testing our weapons on the dummy targets presented by an artificial asteroid shower which is 100% controllable and poses no threat to this colony OR your docked or parked outside space ships.
We are also accompanied by the several cruise liners that have brought tourists to watch the show, and we definitely do not want something bad to happen to them !
So, without any further delay - let us start !
Operator, please launch the meteorites and activate the system in proximity defence mode !
Operator: Sir, asteroids launched, system operational, tracking 1200 fast moving targets.
Zachary: Very well - activate defense.
Operator: All systems green.
buzzing sounds and trembling
As you can hear, the defensive systems are starting to work. Long range lasers, medium range missile systems and short range autocannons and machine guns - everything is working fine. They are ready to track and destroy any moving target within their control zone.
Vince: Sorry sir, did you say ANY ? Because that cruise ship has just started moving.
Zachary: What ? Operator !?
Operator: System has detected movement ! The liner has been hit by a small asteroid and is starting to maneuver away !
Zachary: Tell them to stop ! Disable the system !
Operator: I can not sir ! The system has locked the target and is figuring out firing solutions !
Zachary: WHATEVER YOU HAVE TO DO - DO IT ! NOW ! STOP IT !
Operator: I … can not !
sounds of weapons firing
Blinding explosion outside
Vince: You have seen it all ladies and gentlemen. There is nothing more to show. Our duty as human beings right now is to try and help rescue people from that cruise ship. There were hundreds of people …
Zachary: This was a sabotage ! Sabotage, you hear ! Everyone should know - it is because we helped the CORE ! THEY did not want that to happen - they threatened us ! I will tell the truth ! Everyone will know ! Sabotage !
